gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/191)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/191)
text: مِمَّا أَوْصَى بِهِ آدَمُ ( عليه السلام ) هِبَةَ اللَّهِ أَنْ قَالَ لَهُ عَلَيْكَ بِالرُّمَّانِ فَإِنَّكَ إِنْ أَكَلْتَهُ وَ أَنْتَ جَائِعٌ أَجْزَأَكَ وَ إِنْ أَكَلْتَهُ وَ أَنْتَ شَبْعَانُ أَمْرَأَكَ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Dahqan, from Dorost, from Ibrahim Bin Abdul Hameed,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘From what Adam<sup>as</sup> bequeathed with to Hibtullah<sup>as</sup> was that he<sup>as</sup> said to him<sup>as</sup>: ‘It is upon you with (eating) the Pomegranate, for if you<sup>as</sup> were to eat it and you<sup>as</sup> are hungry so it would suffice you<sup>as</sup>, and if you<sup>as</sup> were to eat it and you<sup>as</sup> are satiated, it would order you (for more food)’.
en.sarwar: 4. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Dihqan from Durust from Ibrahim ibn ‘Abd al-Hamid who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Of the matters that Adam said in his will to Hibbatu Allah was his saying, ‘You must eat pomegranate; if you eat it when you are hungry, it is sufficient; and if you eat when satisfied it is pleasant to you.’”
